name: Add initial webhook receiver
description: A first pass that accepts provider webhooks and writes them to the database. This checkpoint is intentionally broad enough to review the shape of the feature before tightening the edge cases.

name: Verify signatures before persisting
description: The second checkpoint isolates the security review. Reviewers can focus only on request authentication instead of re-reading the whole endpoint.

name: Move parsing into a small adapter
description: This checkpoint turns the endpoint into orchestration code. The diff shows a clean extraction where the parsing rules can be reviewed independently from HTTP handling.

name: Add idempotency and parser tests
description: This checkpoint shows how tests can be reviewed after the production behavior exists. It catches duplicate deliveries and locks down payload normalization.

name: Surface webhook history in the UI
description: The final checkpoint is mostly presentation. Reviewers can compare it against the earlier server checkpoints and verify that stored events become an understandable audit trail.
